summary.what: just release polls the GH release endpoint after tagging, then runs install-release to install the published build as a normal consumer
summary.why: Installing the official published artifact (not the identical local build) sanity-checks the entire release pipeline — CI build, GH release, install.sh — and keeps the local binary current without a manual follow-up
summary.how: Appended a bounded poll loop (gh release view <tag> --jq assets length, ~10m deadline, 15s interval) after git push; on publish runs 'just install-release' and verifies the installed --version contains the tag; on timeout prints recovery hint since the release is already pushed

notes: Considered a cron follow-up (user's initial framing) but a poll-and-install at the tail of 'just release' is simpler with no moving parts — CI can't install on the dev's laptop, so it has to be local anyway. Relied on .goreleaser draft:false so 'latest' equals the just-pushed tag once assets appear; install-release stays the real consumer path (vs pinning VERSION) per the user's 'as if a normal consumer' intent.
